title: carex sororia f. subdivulsa
intro: Carex sororia f. subdivulsa, also known as the woodland sedge, is a subform of the Carex sororia plant species, which belongs to the sedge family and is characterized by its distinctive, flat leaves with a grass-like appearance. Traditionally, Carex sororia f. subdivulsa has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Carex sororia f. subdivulsa can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
